description: Structured evaluation of an asset's confidentiality
Numeric score (1–5) calculated from the CIA assessment.
Categorised risk result: low
Action taken after risk assessment: mitigate
Formal decision to accept a residual risk
The risk remaining after controls are applied.
The raw risk before any controls are applied.
A measure or safeguard implemented to reduce risk.
A weakness in a system that can be exploited by a threat.
Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures — a public identifier for a specific security flaw. Do not translate the acronym.
Common Vulnerability Scoring System — a standard for rating vulnerability severity. Do not translate the acronym.
Common Platform Enumeration — a standardised naming scheme for software, hardware and operating systems.
A security event that has actually occurred and negatively affects assets or operations.
Adherence to applicable laws
A structured set of standards or best practices (e.g. ISO 27001).
A formal management document that defines requirements for a specific area.
A non-mandatory recommendation document providing guidance.
A step-by-step instruction document for performing a process.
A formal attestation of compliance or qualification issued by an authority.
An external organisation supplying products or services.
An external party providing ongoing services within the supply chain.
The network of external suppliers and service providers relevant to NIS-2 compliance.
The logical structure showing relationships and dependencies between assets.
The phases an asset passes through: evaluation
The process of permanently retiring an asset from active operation.
The current state of security updates applied to an asset (up-to-date
The date after which no security updates are provided for a product.
Applying security configuration guidelines (e.g. CIS benchmarks, BSI SiSyP) to reduce attack surface.
The three core dimensions of information security: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability.
Ensuring information is accessible only to authorised entities.
Ensuring information is accurate and complete and has not been tampered with.
Ensuring authorised users have access to information when needed.
Target time to restore a system after failure. Do not translate the abbreviation.
Maximum tolerable data loss expressed as time. Do not translate the abbreviation.
Target service level to maintain during emergency operations. Do not translate the abbreviation.
Maximum duration a system can be down before critical business impact occurs. Do not translate the abbreviation.
Processes ensuring critical operations continue during and after a disruptive event.
Assessment of the effects that interruptions to operations would have.
Observable signals that suggest an attack is underway or imminent. Do not translate the abbreviation.
A specific use of personal data; recorded in the Record of Processing Activities (VVT).
GDPR-mandated register of all data processing activities. Use 'VVT' as the abbreviation in German context.
GDPR Art. 35 — formal assessment when processing is likely to result in high risk to individuals.
The entity that determines the purposes and means of personal data processing.
An entity that processes personal data on behalf of the controller.
Contract between controller and processor required under GDPR Art. 28.
Security measures implemented to protect personal data. Use the abbreviation TOMs.
General Data Protection Regulation (EU). Use 'DSGVO' in German, 'GDPR' in English.
Datenschutz-Grundverordnung — German name for the GDPR. Use in German-language contexts only.
EU Directive 2022/2555 on the security of network and information systems. Keep the hyphen.
International standard for information security management systems. Do not translate.
Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik — German Federal Office for Information Security.
BSI standard for baseline protection of IT systems. Do not translate.
BSI Cloud Computing Compliance Controls Catalogue. Do not translate.
Trusted Information Security Assessment Exchange — automotive industry security standard.
Digital Operational Resilience Act — EU regulation for financial entities. Keep as 'DORA', do not translate.
EU Regulation on Artificial Intelligence. Do not translate.
The main overview screen of the application. Do not translate.
A feature that can be enabled or disabled in the application settings.
A set of permissions assigned to a user (admin, editor, viewer, auditor, etc.).
A specific access right granted to a role.
A chronological record of user actions and system events.
A scheduled notification to review or update a record.
A periodic evaluation to check currency and accuracy of a record.
A high-level summary report for management.
A request from a data subject to exercise their rights under GDPR.
A documented flow of personal or sensitive data between systems or parties.
A simulated cyberattack to identify security weaknesses.
Chief Information Security Officer. Do not translate the acronym.
Data Protection Officer. Do not translate the acronym.
